steps.title: Compare daily amounts
steps.content: The northern right whale can consume up to 24,000 kg of krill per day. A pygmy right whale can weigh up to 3,500 kg, and baleen whales can eat up to 30 percent of their mass, or about 1,050 kg per day.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A invents food-supply competition. Choice B wrongly gives both species the 24,000 kg figure. Choice D invents a difference in how likely the two whales are to eat krill. The remaining comparison follows from the numbers. The answer is C.
